description:
Goal: build the compiler2-owned scope-local publication boundary over quoted source forms. Literal source, macro-produced source, and future compiler-host submissions must all define modules, functions, macros, types, protocols, imports, aliases, and requires by going through one in-progress scope session that reads Fz-shaped quoted roots and publishes the existing compiler2 fact families.

Signal:
- literal source that does not use macros still publishes the same downstream facts as today: `ModuleIndexed`, `CodeScoped`, `ModuleDefined`, `FunctionSource`, `FunctionDefined`, `TypeDefined` waits, `ProtocolDispatch`, and function type-reference wait sets
- one compiler-owned scope session/publisher carries the mutable in-progress scope state for a scope walk: `ScopeSnapshot`, current namespace head, exports, reads/waits/outputs, revision floor, and local protocol context
- `def`, `defmodule`, `defmacro`, `@type`, `@spec`, `defprotocol`, and `defimpl` are handled through that one source-publication boundary over Fz-shaped source readers/cursors
- `FunctionSource` remains the lazy authoritative function fact; `FunctionDefined` remains a derived-on-demand callable surface fact
- `jobs/source` becomes an orchestration caller of the publication boundary rather than the place where publication rules are duplicated inline
- downstream body/dispatch/semantic/artifact jobs do not learn whether a definition was literal source, returned by a macro, or submitted through a future compiler-host call
- no publication path requires a repeated Fz-value -> Rust AST -> Fz-value conversion loop

Change:
- extract the source-publication rules out of `jobs/source.rs` into a dedicated compiler2 module focused on scope-local publication
- model publication around an in-progress scope session rather than ad hoc world-helper calls: alias/import/require binding, function-source noting, type noting, protocol/protocol-impl handling, and child-module discovery/scoping all mutate the same session state
- build the source-publication boundary on the source-root job model from `.11.7.9`, not on old `Rc<Item>`/`FnDef` state
- centralize function/module/type/protocol definition publication so literal source and future macro-produced source use the same path
- consume caller/module projections from the shared `ScopeSnapshot` in `.11.7.3`; the publication boundary must not invent a second env authority of its own
- persist function source roots and source identities as owned compiler2 facts/state keyed by `FunctionId`; function source revision remains driven by quoted transport identity, while code/module surface revision remains semantic-fingerprint-based where already established
- preserve existing type-reference recording and contract/type-definition waits at the publication boundary
- keep import/alias/require source-form handling in this layer, while macro-provider waits and imported macro availability remain `.11.7.6`
- expose a narrow compiler-owned API that a future compile-time host can call into, but do not mix interpreter/runtime wiring into this ticket
- keep protocol type facts and dispatch facts as facts, without folding protocol-domain cleanup from `fz-rh2.12.5` into this ticket

Why:
- Elixir-style `def` works because the compiler remains the authority; macros produce or submit source definitions, but the compiler stores them
- the mutable thing here is the in-progress scope, so the authority belongs in a scope-local publication session rather than in scattered world helper calls
- keeping interpreter/host wiring out of this ticket preserves a crisp seam: `.4` owns compiler publication, `.5` owns running macro code and letting it call back into that publication boundary
title: Compiler2 compiler services: scope-local publication from quoted source forms
